title: A Super-Fast Swimmer Is Switching Teams — and It Could Shake Up College Swimming
summary: Imagine being one of the fastest swimmers in the country as a freshman. That's Teagan O'Dell! She just finished her first year at the University of California, Berkeley, and she was amazing. At the big NCAA championship meet, she made the top 8 in three different races. She got 2nd place in the 200 IM, 3rd in the 400 IM, and 6th in the 200 backstroke. She scored 46 points all by herself — the second-most of any freshman. Now, Teagan has decided to transfer to the University of Virginia. Why is that a big deal? Virginia has won the national team title six years in a row! Adding Teagan makes them even stronger. She's really good at the IM, which is a race where you swim all four strokes: butterfly, backstroke, breaststroke, and freestyle. Teagan said she's super excited to join the "Cavalier family" and thanked her Cal coaches and teammates for an amazing year. She'll start swimming for Virginia this fall. With her help, Virginia will try to win its seventh straight NCAA championship. That's like winning the Super Bowl seven times in a row! Keep an eye on Teagan O'Dell — she's a star.
why_it_matters: A star athlete’s choice to switch schools shows how making the right team fit can open up new chances to win big championships, something every young athlete dreams about.

keywords.explanation: A student in their first year of college or high school.
The National Collegiate Athletic Association, the main group that organizes college sports championships in the U.S.
The final big event or series of games that decides the best team or athlete in a sport.
A swimming stroke done on your back, moving your arms in a circle while kicking your legs.
When a student or athlete leaves one school and goes to another.
The team name (mascot) for sports at the University of Virginia.

questions.question: What place did Teagan O'Dell get in the 200 IM at the NCAA meet?
How many total points did Teagan score by herself at the NCAA championship?
Which university is Teagan transferring to?
How many years in a row has Virginia won the national team title?
Which stroke is NOT one of the four in an IM race?
What did Teagan call the Virginia team?

background_read: The NCAA (National Collegiate Athletic Association) is the main organization for college sports in the United States, hosting national championships in swimming and many other sports.
The University of Virginia has become a swimming powerhouse, winning the NCAA women’s team championship six years in a row as of early 2025.
College athletes sometimes transfer to another school if they feel it’s a better fit for their skills or if they want to be part of a stronger program.
Article_Structure: WHO: Teagan O’Dell, a freshman swimmer at the University of California, Berkeley, who recently decided to leave.
WHAT: She is transferring to the University of Virginia, which has won six straight national team titles, making them even stronger.
WHERE: From UC Berkeley in California to the University of Virginia.
WHY: To join a dominant team and have a better chance at winning a national championship; she said she is excited to be part of the 'Cavalier family.'

perspectives.description: Fans and coaches at Virginia are thrilled to add such a talented freshman who will help the team try for a seventh straight national title.
California loses one of its best young swimmers, which might hurt their team’s performance at future competitions.
Transfers are becoming more common in college sports as athletes look for the best opportunity to develop and win, much like free agency in professional leagues.
